findings.summary: Voltage divider feedback networks for boost regulator U2 (R17+R18) and LDO U3 (R27+R28) not detected; Missing regulator_caps observation for U2 (MT3608 boost converter) in Rev.A1 output; Decoupling...

incorrect.description: The ERC no_driver warnings report pin names from the custom library component that do not correspond to the net label that routes to them. For example, net 'START' is reported as connecting to U1 pin 10 with pin_name 'CS#', and net 'CS#' connects to U1 pin 9 with pin_name 'START'. Net 'DRDY#' shows pin_name 'SCK' and net 'MISO' shows pin_name 'DIN'. Net 'PWDN#' shows pin_name 'AVDD' with type power_in, which is clearly wrong for a reset/power-down control signal. These are mismatches between library pin numbering and the schematic net labels — the net assignments are correct in the schematic but the pin_name metadata in ERC output is misleading and likely to confuse downstream consumers of the JSON.

missed.description: The schematic contains two resistor divider feedback networks: R17 (9.1K_1%) + R18 (1.05K_1%) set the output voltage of the MT3608 boost converter (U2), and R27 (5.6K_1%) + R28 (1.05K_1%) set the output voltage of the AP2127K-ADJ LDO (U3). Both 'voltage_dividers' and 'feedback_networks' arrays are empty in the output. These are textbook voltage-setting feedback dividers on adjustable regulators and should be detected.

The Rev.A output correctly generates a 'regulator_caps' design observation for both U2 (MT3608 switching) and U3 (AP2127K-ADJ LDO). The Rev.A1 output only generates the observation for U3 but omits U2. In both schematic versions the boost converter U2 has its input/output capacitors on unnamed internal nets, so U2's regulator_caps observation should also be flagged in Rev.A1. The difference appears to be a net-numbering side-effect where Rev.A1 resolves the MT3608 input differently.

The boost converter (U2/MT3608) has C18 (22uF/6.3V) on the input and C19 (22uF/10V) on the output. The LDO (U3/AP2127K-ADJ) has C21 (1uF) on its output. Because these capacitors sit on unnamed internal nets rather than named power rails (+5VA, +3.3VD), they are entirely absent from the decoupling_analysis. The analysis reports only 2 caps on +5VA (C3 100nF + C22 1uF) and 1 cap on +3.3VD (C2 100nF), missing the bulk storage caps for the boost/LDO stages. This leads to a misleading picture of decoupling coverage.

findings.summary: Voltage divider feedback networks for boost regulator U2 (R17+R18) and LDO U3 (R27+R28) not detected; Decoupling analysis misses bulk output capacitors C18 (22uF) and C19 (22uF) for the boost conve...

correct.description: The SPI bus (MISO/MOSI/SCK/CS#) is correctly detected with U1 (ADS1262) as the device. Power rails +3.3VD, +5VA, GNDA, GNDD are all correctly identified. All 26 components are counted accurately with correct type breakdown: 6 resistors, 9 capacitors, 3 ICs, 3 connectors, 1 inductor, 2 diodes, 1 ferrite bead, 1 jumper. Both boost converter (U2/MT3608) and LDO (U3/AP2127K-ADJ) are detected as power regulators.

incorrect.description: Identical to Rev.A1: net 'START' shows U1 pin 10 with pin_name 'CS#', net 'CS#' shows U1 pin 9 with pin_name 'START', net 'DRDY#' shows pin_name 'SCK', net 'MISO' shows pin_name 'DIN', and net 'PWDN#' shows pin_name 'AVDD' with type power_in. The custom ADS1262 library symbol uses non-standard pin numbering causing the analyzer to retrieve the wrong pin names from the library data when reporting ERC warnings.

missed.description: Identical to Rev.A1: R17 (12.1K_1%) + R18 (1.05K_1%) form the feedback voltage divider for MT3608 boost converter U2, and R27 (5.6K_1%) + R28 (1.05K_1%) form the feedback divider for AP2127K-ADJ LDO U3. Both 'voltage_dividers' and 'feedback_networks' arrays are empty. The mid-point of each divider connects back to the regulator's feedback pin — a classic adjustable regulator feedback network that should be detected.

Same as Rev.A1: C18 (22uF/6.3V), C19 (22uF/10V), and C21 (1uF) all sit on unnamed internal power nets and are omitted from the decoupling_analysis. Only C3 (100nF on +5VA), C22 (1uF on +5VA), and C2 (100nF on +3.3VD) are reported. The 22uF bulk storage caps for the switching and LDO regulators are invisible to the analysis.
